rulings.Case Title: JEAN-YVES LENDORMY VS. KYLE HANSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy'S Motion To Correct Amend Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 4, 2019, Line 1, Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy'S Motion To Correct Amend Judgment Lendormy's motion to amend judgment is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Hanson and Murphy present evidence that the first time they received Lendormy's costs memorandum was when they were served with this motion. (McElhenney Dec., par. 2.) Pursuant to CCP sections 473 and 128(a)(8), Hanson and Murphy have until January 14, 2019 to file a motion to tax costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Hanson and Murphy is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: JEAN-YVES LENDORMY VS. KYLE HANSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kyle Hanson, Mark Murphy'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1285 And 1286.2
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 4, 2019, Line 1, Defendants Kyle Hanson and Mark Murphy'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1285 And 1286.2 Hanson and Murphy's petition to vacate arbitration award is DENIED. First, the court lacks jurisdiction to entertain the petition because the court already entered judgment in this case. "The entry of judgment ordinarily terminates a trial court's jurisdiction to rule on the merits of a case, apart from ruling on a new trial motion, a motion to vacate the judgment or a similar motion." (Ballona Wetlands Land Trust v. City of Los Angeles (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 455, 479.) Second, on November 20, 2018, Hanson and Murphy appealed this court's order confirming the arbitration award and the ensuing judgment. Per CCP § 916, this court no longer has jurisdiction to grant the petition to vacate. The petition to vacate is a proceeding "embraced therein or affected" by the order confirming arbitration award and judgment. (CCP § 916(a).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Lendormy is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: JOSE RODRIGUEZ VS. THOMAS H. TASHJIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff Jose Ramirez
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 4, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT FOLEY CONSTRUCTION'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff Jose Ramirez. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Given the alleged nature of the serious ongoing physical injuries resulting in disability, and the plaintiff's expert disclosure of multiple treating pain management specialists, the Court finds good cause for a physical examination by a pain management physician. The exam shall occur on either January 7 or 21, or on a mutually agreeable date selected by the parties. Plaintiff's counsel, or another observer such as a nurse, and/or a court reporter may attend and record the exam via audio or stenographic means. The examining physician, Dr. Jayson Hymes, shall not repeat any questioning of the plaintiff concerning how the subject accident happened if this has previously been covered in plaintiff's deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ASHLEY RUST VS. SENDHUB, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 4, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANTs SENDHUB, INC., and CAMEO GLOBAL, INC.,'s Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs Defendants' motion to strike or tax costs is DENIED. CCP §685.040 et seq. ("Right to Costs of Enforcing Judgment") applies; CRC 3.1700 ("Prejudgment costs") does not. The costs memorandum was timely (CCP §685.070(b)), and attorney fees may be sought via such a memo. (David S. Karton, A Law Corp. v. Dougherty (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 133, 145.) Plaintiffs evidenced reasonable attorney fees - a simple showing that could have been addressed in defendants' reply. (Weiss Dec.) Defendants' counsel controlled the timing of submissions by selecting a hearing date for their motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 4, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT YANG DDS, INC. Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination Of Plaintiff. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Case Title: MAGDALENA LANUZA VS. ACCENTCARE INC. A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Paga Settlement And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 4, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFF MAGDALENA LANUZA's Motion To Approve Paga Settlement And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Plaintiff Magdalena Lanuza's motion "for an order approving the settlement of the PAGA claim alleged against Defendants AccentCare, Inc. and AccentCare Home Health of California, Inc¿.in accordance with Labor Code §2699(l)" is GRANTED. No opposition was filed. I note that, in the (operative) first amended complaint, Ms. Lanuza sued solely "on behalf of the State of California, as a private attorney general" and pled no individual claims. Thus, the motion is granted as expressly requested in the notice of motion; that notice does not mention Ms. Lanuza's individual claims, over which I appear to have no jurisdiction. rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bellrose, Llc, A California Limited Liability, John Kaufman Notice Of Motion And Defendant John D. Kaufmans Motion To Compel Responses Re Defendant John D. Kaufmans Requests For Production Of Documents And Inspection Of Premises [To Plaintiff Damien Quinn], Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 4, 2019, Line 3, Defendants Bellrose, LLC, John Kaufman'S Motion To Compel Responses Re Defendant John D. Kaufmans Requests For Production Of Documents And Inspection Of Premises [To Plaintiff Damien Quinn], Set One. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties are directed to bring to the hearing documentation sufficient for the court to determine: 1) when was a verified response to the document demand served? and 2) what are the precise discovery requests and responses at issue on this motion? If the Court is convinced that the verified response was served more than 45 days before the motion was filed, the Court may well conclude that it has lost jurisdiction to hear the motion. Sanctions requested by moving party will be denied to due to failure to comply with CCP sec. 2023.040 (failure to identify persons against whom sanctions sought). The opposing papers seem to request sanctions, but also have failed to identify the person(s) against whom sanctions are sought, and hence sanctions must be denied as a procedural matter without reaching the merits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. RON WHITEHEAD JR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 4, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT RON WHITEHEAD's Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Defendant Ron Whitehead, Jr.'s motion to quash is GRANTED. It is Bank of the West's burden to establish sufficient minimum contacts for the court to exercise jurisdiction over Whitehead. (See Mihlon v. Sup. Crt. (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 703, 710.) For specific jurisdiction, "the crucial inquiry concerns the character of defendant's activity in the forum, whether the cause of action arises out of or has a substantial connection with that activity, and upon the balancing of the convenience of the parties and the interests of the state in assuming jurisdiction." (Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc., (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 448 (italics in original).) In this case, Bank of the West's citation to the complaint and the declaration of counsel Cefalu is insufficient to demonstrate jurisdiction. The underlying transaction has no nexus to California. Whitehead resides in Texas, he travelled to Tennessee to discuss purchase/lease of the PDS printer, and entered into a financing agreement with Connext (an Indiana entity) that contemplated that the printer would be shipped to Texas. (Whitehead Dec., pars. 1-7.) Bank of the West cannot manufacture jurisdiction by subsequently becoming an assignee of Connext. Whitehead's contacts with California (attending trade shows, selling denim in California) are irrelevant to the instant action. Those contacts have no relationship to the claims that form the basis of this action. The court exercises its discretion and denies Bank of the West's request for jurisdictional discovery. (See HealthMarkets, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1160, 1173.) Bank of the West does not highlight what discovery it would like to conduct or what relevant facts it hopes to unearth to establish jurisdiction. As stated, the fact that Whitehead may engage in unrelated business transactions in California over the internet is irrelevant to the specific jurisdiction analysis. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Whitehead, Jr. is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: FAST TRACK INVESTMENT COMPANY LLC A DELAWARE VS. LESLEY REED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 4, 2019, Line 14, DEFENDANT JOHN FITZPATRICK VANNUCCI IN PRO PER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Plaintiff does not dispute that a complaint is an improper vehicle to demand arbitration, a writ of attachment and/or a temporary restraining order, so the demurrers are SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to the second and fifth "causes of action." Defendant John Vannuchi's demurrers are otherwise OVERRULED. Breaches of contract and fiduciary duty are adequately pled, based in part on Vannuchi's signing the "acknowledgement by counsel" to hold settlement proceeds on plaintiff's behalf. The complaint also alleges Vannuchi had possession of plaintiff's funds but did not pay them, which adequately pleads conversion.
